Notes: Took the Thais corner straight up to the ledge at the start of the traverse this time. A fun variation! Started raining before I started the traverse then only got worse. We only felt it about half way through pitch 3, but were soaked by the time we both made it to the summit!
Literally the fastest I've guided three clients on Old Mans! On the ground by noon after summitting
Heard a cool pileated woodpecker in a nearby tree from the top!
Guiding my cient up here to prep for the grand in approach shoes!
TR to check my clients gear placements
TR to check my clients gear placements
Setting up a TR for my clients to mock lead. Good spot for it!

Had a spider rappell off the tree at the top of the last pitch about a foot from my face and got to watch him slowly make his way down while I was belaying. Neat! My client also helped remove a really spooky loose block in the middle of the crux. 15 pounds-ish worth of rock, he pulled it out and held it while I lowered him to the ledge to stash it somewhere.

What a zoo, people everywhere, but we were able to flow through without any holdups! Saw some craziness unfolding while we rappelled Beesting, some folks doing some weird shit rappelling down P1 of Skyline after doing Candy Corner? Weird.
TR to clean juuuust before some wicked rain blew through!
Counterbalance lower to clean the anchors

Had triple generational clients, and the little girl couldn't take it and I had to lower her back down, then rap Conn's back to the ground. Ended up taking her and her grandmother caving in The Womb while dad went up with Holly's crew!
Against all odds, I bootied MY OWN nut tool that a client lost LAST YEAR! Just fished it out from under some leaves in the bottom of the wide crack on the ramp. Schweet!
Saw some cute lil' owlets just off Roy Gap road on the walk up. Soooo cuteeee!!!
Sopping wet first pitch, then rained on my follower the whole time he was coming up the second pitch! Still goes though
Got SUPER WINDY as we were finishing up, which brought in some rain! Love having my painters tarp handy
Easily bootied a big blue DMM Offset, but my pink tricam got taken by the mountain gods. Truly, the mountain giveth and the mountain taketh away.
Met a territorial wasp right near the top, so I had to traverse around him.. Had good pro to keep the clients safe even with the detour!
Placed a brown tricam like 3 inches away from literally the biggest spider I have ever seen at Seneca. He was fine with it though. Poor guy only had six legs.
TRd P1 twice to check client gear and clean
In approach shoes while still a little damp from rain in the morning. Exciting!
Counterbalanced lower to get my client and gear down from The Burn
Saw a ZILLION turkey vultures circling while rapping the route. They left as quickly as they appeared too!
Been a while since I climbed slab like this, definitely had to dial in the mental game on the runout! Crux move was fun as ever
Clip the last piece (#2) direct, no need for a draw
Counterbalance lower to clean the route
Holy rope drag, batman! It's legit better to just solo this sumbitch
Fixed the rope drag issue! Also nice to extend the belay
Found a nice way to split this into two pitches. Thanks Tim!
TR solo to clean everything up after getting the ledges down and getting everyones mock aid leads done
Setting up ropes for everyone to jug and set up the portaledges. Really rough day today, but we ended up with two ledges and two folks sleeping up there
TR solo in approach shoes to clean after teaching aid climbing all day
Counterbalance lower to clean the route
Always so fun to guide this!
Found a neat way to extend the belay in order to hang out at the top of the last corner. Gold DMM Offset in a shallow crack to elevate the master point is key
Had to bail at the top of P1 after my first client got up there so we could all respond to an accident at Banana that took the rest of the day. Rough
Probably the fastest I've guided three clients up Old Man's
Hanging belay is rough with 3 followers
What an incredible climb, probably the most underrated climb at Seneca!
Finally found beta that makes this feel super cruiser
SO cruiser, what an awesome rock climb
Spooked a bat in a crack, luckily he didn’t bite me!
Two counterbalance lowers to get the job done! What an awesome route

Had to redo pitch 2 to get a nut my follower couldn't get from the very start, then had to redo pitch 3 because my follower couldn't get a locker open at the anchor. Got caught in a bad thunderstorm right at the tail end of the PO rap!
Followed P1 and P2, then led P3 to save some time
Following Coop on his first real trad lead!
Following my client on a mock multipitch
Cleaning REAL FAST bc of repeated rockfall from someone above
Setting up a TR for my client to mock lead
TR soloed to get back up to the West Pole rap anchors to do a tandem rappel after a bit of a freakout on rappelling occurred
Minor freakout at the top of P2 just before the big ledge. Scampered down and showed him how to hand jam to get up which he surprisingly actually learned even after the freakout
Counterbalance lower to clean the route
Guided my oldest client yet at 81! First time on the upper pitches
Guiding to set up silks from a highline across the notch. Two pitches from upper broadway to the highline anchor.
Guiding. Client bootied a fixed nut!
Guiding. � TR pitches to anchor stuff and clean
Guiding. Just the last two pitches after rapping Conn's
Led P1 to set up a TR. Guiding
Guiding, shouldn't have done the third pitch
Guiding. LRS with Revo. Just P1.
Guiding. Phone rescue mission from the crack under the first summit rappel station. Success!
Guiding. Just the last two pitches.
Guiding. Busy day on the rock!
Guiding. Followed Kyle's lead, guided Alex and Kolton
Guiding, accidentally took the 5.6/7 roof on the left just below the anchor. Oops!
